title: Hot and Sour Soup

seo.title: Authentic Hot and Sour Soup Recipe - Easy & Delicious

seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Hot and Sour Soup with mushrooms, tofu, and bamboo shoots
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of homemade Hot and Sour Soup, bursting with traditional Chinese flavors.

description: Experience the quintessential flavors of Chinese cuisine with this robust Hot and Sour Soup. A delightful harmony of spicy chili, tangy vinegar, and savory ingredients creates a comforting and invigorating bowl. Perfect for a chilly evening or as a zesty appetizer to any meal.

ingredients.notes: mixed with 3 tbsp cold water to form a slurry

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rehydrate dried shiitake and wood ear mushrooms in hot water for 20 minutes, then drain, stem shiitake, and slice all mushrooms thinly. Slice pork tenderloin into thin matchsticks, dice tofu, and julienne bamboo shoots. Beat the eggs and prepare the cornstarch slurry.

instructions.title: Simmer Broth and Pork
instructions.text: In a large pot, bring chicken or vegetable broth to a simmer over medium-high heat. Add the sliced pork tenderloin and cook for 3-4 minutes until the pork is no longer pink. Skim off any foam that rises to the surface.

instructions.title: Add Main Vegetables and Seasonings
instructions.text: Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms, wood ear mushrooms, diced tofu, and julienned bamboo shoots to the simmering broth. Stir in soy sauce, 3 tablespoons of rice vinegar, and 1 teaspoon of white pepper. Adjust seasoning to your preference, adding more vinegar or white pepper if desired.

instructions.title: Thicken the Soup
instructions.text: Stir the cornstarch slurry well and slowly pour it into the simmering soup while continuously stirring. Continue to stir gently until the soup thickens to your desired consistency. This should take about 1-2 minutes.

instructions.title: Incorporate Eggs
instructions.text: Reduce heat to low. Slowly drizzle the beaten eggs into the soup in a thin stream while gently stirring in one direction. This will create delicate egg ribbons. Do not stir too vigorously or the eggs will break up too much.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the pot from heat. Stir in the remaining 1-2 tablespoons of rice vinegar, sesame oil, and optional chili oil. Taste and adjust any final seasonings. Ladle the hot soup into bowls and garnish with sliced green onions before serving immediately.

tips: For a truly authentic taste, use good quality Chinese black vinegar (Chinkiang vinegar) for its deep, complex tang. It offers a more nuanced flavor than regular rice vinegar.
Adjust the levels of white pepper and vinegar to your personal preference for spiciness and sourness. A little extra chili oil can boost the heat without altering the sour balance.
Hot and Sour Soup is best served immediately. If reheating, add a splash of broth or water as it may thicken further, and re-check seasoning levels.
article:
The world of Chinese cuisine is a vibrant tapestry of flavors, textures, and aromas, and among its most beloved masterpieces stands the iconic Hot and Sour Soup. This comforting, complex, and utterly satisfying dish has captivated palates worldwide, offering a unique dance between piquant heat and tangy sourness, all enveloped in a savory broth. Whether enjoyed as a starter at a bustling dim sum restaurant or a warming meal on a chilly evening, Hot and Sour Soup delivers an unforgettable culinary experience that transcends mere sustenance. It’s a testament to the artistry of balancing bold flavors, creating a symphony that is both invigorating and deeply soothing.

## What is Hot and Sour Soup?

At its heart, Hot and Sour Soup is a traditional Chinese soup renowned for its distinctive flavor profile: spicy, sour, savory, and umami-rich. The "hot" typically comes from white pepper and, often, chili oil, while the "sour" is derived from rice vinegar. Beyond these primary elements, the soup is a delightful medley of textures. Common ingredients include shredded pork (though vegetarian versions are popular), silken tofu, wood ear mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and sometimes lily flowers, all suspended in a thickened, flavorful broth. The final touch is usually a swirl of whisked egg, creating delicate, silken ribbons throughout the soup. Originating from the Szechuan and Hunan provinces of China, where bold and spicy flavors are king, the quintessential Hot and Sour Soup has evolved with regional variations, but its core appeal lies in its dynamic and harmonious taste. It’s a dish that warms you from the inside out, stimulating the senses with every spoonful.

## Ingredients for Hot and Sour Soup

Crafting your own Hot and Sour Soup at home allows for personalization and ensures the freshest flavors. Here’s a breakdown of the essential components you’ll need:

*   **Broth:** Approximately 6 cups of good quality chicken or vegetable broth. This forms the flavorful base.
*   **Protein:** 4-6 ounces of pork loin or tenderloin, thinly shredded. For vegetarian versions, extra firm tofu, cut into small strips or cubes, works wonderfully.
*   **Tofu:** 8 ounces silken or firm tofu, cut into small, thin strips or cubes.
*   **Mushrooms:** 1/2 cup dried wood ear mushrooms (rehydrated and thinly sliced) or fresh shiitake mushrooms (stems removed, thinly sliced).
*   **Bamboo Shoots:** 1/2 cup canned bamboo shoots, drained and thinly sliced.
*   **Aromatics:** 1 tablespoon fresh ginger, minced; 2 cloves garlic, minced (optional, but adds depth).
*   **Seasonings:**
    *   1/4 cup light soy sauce
    *   1/4 cup rice vinegar (or black vinegar for a deeper flavor)
    *   1-2 tablespoons white pepper (adjust to your heat preference – this is crucial!)
    *   1 teaspoon sesame oil
    *   1-2 teaspoons chili oil or paste (optional, for extra heat)
*   **Thickener:** 3 tablespoons cornstarch mixed with 1/4 cup cold water (cornstarch slurry).
*   **Egg:** 1 large egg, lightly beaten.
*   **Garnish:** Green onions, thinly sliced, and fresh cilantro (optional).

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Hot and Sour Soup at home is surprisingly straightforward, yielding incredibly rewarding results.

1.  **Prepare Ingredients:** Rehydrate wood ear mushrooms if using dried, then slice them thinly along with the bamboo shoots, pork (or tofu), and regular tofu. Mince your ginger and garlic. Prepare your cornstarch slurry and lightly beat your egg.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics (Optional):** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat a tablespoon of cooking oil over medium heat. Add minced ginger and garlic (if using) and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
3.  **Add Broth and Protein:** Pour in the chicken or vegetable broth. Bring to a gentle simmer. Add the shredded pork and cook until no longer pink, about 2-3 minutes. If using tofu as the main protein, add it now.
4.  **Introduce Vegetables and Seasonings:** Add the sliced wood ear mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and tofu. Stir in the light soy sauce, rice vinegar, and white pepper. Taste and adjust the white pepper and vinegar to your preference – remember, balance is key for Hot and Sour Soup. If you like it spicier, add chili oil or paste now.
5.  **Thicken the Soup:** Give your cornstarch slurry a quick whisk to ensure it’s well mixed. Slowly pour the slurry into the simmering soup in a thin stream, stirring continuously. Continue to stir until the soup thickens to your desired consistency. Let it simmer for another minute or two to cook out any raw cornstarch taste.
6.  **Create Egg Ribbons:** Remove the soup from direct heat or lower it to a very gentle simmer. Slowly drizzle the lightly beaten egg into the soup in a thin stream, while gently stirring the soup with chopsticks or a fork in a circular motion. This creates delicate, wispy egg ribbons. Do not stir vigorously immediately after adding the egg, allow it a moment to cook.
7.  **Finish and Serve:** Stir in the sesame oil. Ladle the Hot and Sour Soup into bowls. Garnish generously with sliced green onions and a sprinkle of fresh cilantro if desired. Serve immediately.

## Tips for the Best Hot and Sour Soup

Achieving the perfect balance in your Hot and Sour Soup is an art. Here are some tips to elevate your homemade version:

*   **Taste and Adjust:** This is paramount. The "hot" and "sour" components are highly personal. Start with the recommended amounts of white pepper and vinegar, then taste frequently and adjust to your liking. You can always add more, but you can't take it away!
*   **Quality Ingredients:** A good quality broth makes a significant difference. Fresh ginger and good rice vinegar are also key.
*   **White Pepper is King:** Don't skimp on the white pepper. It provides a unique, pungent heat that is distinct from chili heat and is essential to the authentic flavor of Hot and Sour Soup.
*   **Cornstarch Slurry Technique:** Ensure your cornstarch is fully dissolved in cold water before adding it to the hot soup. Pour it in slowly while stirring constantly to prevent lumps and to control the thickness. Let the soup simmer for a minute or two after thickening to cook off any starchy taste.
*   **Egg Ribbons:** For fine, delicate egg ribbons, make sure your soup is at a gentle simmer, not a rolling boil. Drizzle the egg in a thin stream from a height, and stir slowly in one direction as you pour.
*   **Vinegar Timing:** Some chefs prefer to add a final splash of vinegar right before serving to brighten the sour notes, as prolonged cooking can diminish its tang.

## Serving Suggestions

Hot and Sour Soup is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways:

*   **Appetizer:** It makes a fantastic starter for any Chinese meal, whetting the appetite without being overly filling.
*   **Light Meal:** Paired with some steamed rice or a couple of spring rolls, a large bowl of Hot and Sour Soup can be a satisfying light lunch or dinner.
*   **Comfort Food:** On its own, it’s a deeply comforting dish, perfect for a cold day or when you’re feeling under the weather.
*   **Garnishes:** Always offer fresh garnishes like thinly sliced green onions and a sprinkle of fresh cilantro. For those who like extra heat, a small dish of chili oil or a dash of black vinegar can be placed on the table for individual customization.
*   **Pairing:** It complements a wide array of Chinese dishes, from fried rice and noodles to stir-fries and roasted meats, providing a refreshing contrast to richer flavors.

## Variations & Substitutions

The beauty of Hot and Sour Soup lies in its adaptability. Don't be afraid to experiment!

*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** Easily make it vegetarian by using vegetable broth and omitting the pork. For a vegan version, ensure your soy sauce is vegan-friendly and skip the egg (or use a vegan egg substitute if desired for texture). Increase the amount of tofu and mushrooms for more substance.
*   **Protein Alternatives:** Instead of pork, you can use thinly sliced chicken breast, shrimp, or even ground pork. For a richer flavor, some recipes use dried scallops (rehydrated) or a mix of seafood.
*   **Mushroom Medley:** Experiment with different types of mushrooms like oyster mushrooms, enoki mushrooms, or dried shiitake for varying textures and umami depth.
*   **Vegetable Additions:** Napa cabbage, water chestnuts, carrots, or even snow peas can be added for extra crunch and nutrition.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the white pepper and chili oil to your personal tolerance. For a milder version, reduce both. For an extra kick, add a pinch of Szechuan peppercorns for a numbing sensation.
*   **Sourness:** While rice vinegar is standard, a touch of black vinegar can add a deeper, more complex sour note. Lemon juice can also be used in a pinch for brightness.
*   **Thickening Agents:** Arrowroot powder can be used as an alternative to cornstarch for thickening, providing a slightly clearer soup.

Hot and Sour Soup is more than just a dish; it's an experience. Its intricate balance of flavors, warming properties, and satisfying textures make it a perennial favorite. Whether you stick to the classic recipe or venture into creative variations, mastering this iconic Chinese soup at home is a rewarding culinary journey that will undoubtedly impress and delight. So, gather your ingredients, fire up your stove, and prepare to savor the incredible depth and comfort of homemade Hot and Sour Soup.
